description:
Determine the stall safety margin for a NACA 4412 airfoil (100 panels) at Reynolds Number 1,000,000 and Mach 0, analyzing a range from -5 to 25 degrees alpha. Calculate the stall margin at an operating angle of 10 degrees using the formula: Stall Margin = Stall Angle - 10.0. Create a report at /home/ga/Documents/stall_margin_report.txt following the format below, including the Stall Angle, Maximum Cl, and aerodynamic coefficients at 10 degrees. Provide an assessment of SAFE if the stall margin is greater than 3.0 degrees, otherwise UNSAFE. Save the project to /home/ga/Documents/projects/stall_assessment.wpa.

STALL MARGIN ASSESSMENT REPORT
==============================
Airfoil: NACA 4412
Reynolds Number: 1000000
Operating Angle of Attack: 10.0 deg

RESULTS:
Stall Angle (alpha_stall): <value> deg
Maximum Cl (Cl_max): <value>
Cl at alpha=10: <value>
Cd at alpha=10: <value>
L/D at alpha=10: <value>
Stall Margin: <value> deg

ASSESSMENT: <SAFE or UNSAFE>
